title: CXMT targeting over 500Mb/mm2 with 64-layer 3D DRAM test vehicle
gist: Chinese memory maker CXMT has developed a 64-layer stacked DRAM architecture (Multilayer Horizontal Cell) achieving 525 Mb/mm2 density, placing it within the 1c node range (<600 Mb/mm2) competitive with leading-edge DRAM. The design uses vertical word lines and pillar capacitors with a 0.280 um x 0.435 um unit cell, suggesting China is advancing toward DRAM density parity with Samsung and SK Hynix.

insights.claim: CXMT's 64-layer stacked DRAM test vehicle achieves 525 Mb/mm2 using a 0.280 um x 0.435 um unit cell, placing it in the 1c node density range (<600 Mb/mm2) comparable to leading-edge DRAM from Samsung and SK Hynix.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: China's domestic DRAM capacity is closing the density gap with global leaders, reducing Western suppliers' competitive moat and potentially accelerating China's memory self-sufficiency.
insights.quote: The unit cell is 0.280 um x 0.435 um (a generously wide footprint), which gives 525 Mb/mm2 in 64 layers.

insights.claim: CXMT's Multilayer Horizontal Cell (MHC) 3D DRAM architecture uses vertical word lines and pillar capacitors, achieving an Ion/Ioff ratio of ~1E8 and subthreshold slope of ~93 mV/dec, demonstrating viable transistor performance for stacked DRAM.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Strong transistor electrical characteristics confirm the MHC approach is technically sound, not merely a paper design, raising the credibility of CXMT's path to volume production.
insights.quote: This design boasts an excellent combination of high on-state and off-state current ratio (Ion/Ioff) of ~1E8, a steep subthreshold slope (SS) of ~93 mV/dec and a moderate threshold voltage (Vt) of 0.76 V.

insights.claim: CXMT's 3D DRAM design places CMOS logic under or bonded over the memory array rather than outside it, enabling density competitive with 1c planar DRAM without sacrificing peripheral circuit area.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: This architectural approach is key to achieving high density parity; if scalable to production, it removes one of the main area penalties of 3D stacking.
insights.quote: The CMOS is expected to be under or bonded over the array, not adding extra area outside.

insights.claim: CXMT demonstrated feasibility of a 64-layer 3D DRAM structure through critical unit process development, suggesting a credible roadmap beyond the current 5-layer test vehicle toward high-volume stacked DRAM.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Layer count scalability is the primary risk for 3D DRAM commercialization; demonstrating 64-layer process feasibility lowers that risk significantly.
insights.quote: we have also explored the feasibility of fabricating a 64-layer structure through the development of critical unit processes

themes.theme: China DRAM Self-Sufficiency and Geopolitical Risk

insights.claim: CXMT reaching 1c-range DRAM density via 3D stacking signals that China's domestic memory industry may be developing an alternative path to advanced DRAM that bypasses leading-edge lithography export controls.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: If 3D DRAM can substitute for advanced planar DRAM at competitive density, U.S. export controls on EUV and advanced DUV become less effective at limiting China's memory capabilities.

insights.claim: CXMT's progress on stacked DRAM with density targeting over 500 Mb/mm2 increases competitive pressure on Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung in the commodity DRAM segment if CXMT can scale to volume production.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Investors in Western DRAM makers should monitor CXMT's production ramp timeline as a long-term pricing and market-share risk vector.

insights.claim: Stacked DRAM using multilayer horizontal cells is being pursued as the primary vehicle to continue DRAM bit density scaling beyond what planar 2D DRAM shrinks can achieve.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: 3D DRAM represents a structural shift in memory scaling similar to NAND's transition to 3D, with significant capex and IP implications for all DRAM makers.
insights.quote: Stacked dynamic random access memory (DRAM) technology has garnered considerable attention as a means to continue the relentless pursuit of scaling.

insights.claim: Novel process integrations including BSG/PSG capping doping, double gate formation, and channel thickness optimization are required to achieve viable transistor performance in horizontal-cell 3D DRAM architectures.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: The complexity of these process steps represents a high barrier to entry and manufacturing yield risk, but also IP moats for whoever solves them first at volume.
insights.quote: A novel process integration scheme incorporating boron silicate glass (BSG)/phosphorus silicate glass (PSG) capping doping, double gate formation, channel thickness optimization
